title: hippophae rhamnoides subsp. neurocarpa
intro: Hippophae rhamnoides subsp. neurocarpa is a subspecies of the sea buckthorn plant, known for its vibrant orange berries and thorny branches. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Hippophae rhamnoides subsp. neurocarpa is often used to make herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, its berries are rich in vitamins and flavonoids, making them a popular ingredient in jams, juices, and other food products.
